name: Banded Shoulder Flow (Seated, KB-Day Variant)
description: 12 min. Reusable Tue/Thu morning flow variant — built around a 7-position seated mini-band sequence (@sculptherbody). Sustained tension, NOT reps. Pairs well with KB days because it primes scaps, rear delts, and lats for overhead/rack work without burning the legs. Bookended with the standard thoracic + hip openers from the W7-W8 KB flow.

exercises.notes: 30s each side. Deep thoracic rotation — primes the spine for the band work.

sets.name: Seated Banded Shoulder Flow

exercises.notes: Heels tucked. Arms straight overhead, hands wider than shoulders. **Sustained tension**, ribs down — not snappy reps.
Pull band down behind the head — **elbows drive to ribs**, scaps depress. Don't crank the neck forward.
Band wraps the upper back, hands hold the ends in front. **Press straight out to T** — feel the rear delts and mid-traps.
Band at sternum height, **pull apart and squeeze scaps**. Slower than overhead version — chest opens, ribs stay down.
Sweep arms further back behind the body line. Subtle — **don't overarch the lower back** to chase range.
30s = 15s each side. One arm overhead, other diagonal-out. **Lat + side-body stretch** with band tension. Switch sides.
Both arms in overhead V/Y. **Hold and breathe** — lower traps fire here. Closes the flow.

sets.notes: 2 rounds, ~3 min each. Sustained tension throughout, no rest between positions. Mini-band stays in your hands the whole time.

exercises.notes: 20s each side. Press into the stretch.

tips: **KB-day variant** — designed to pair with Tue/Thu KB sessions where upper-body skill work dominates.
**Sustained tension, not reps.** Each banded position = ~20s steady pull, breathe through it.
Mini-band only — no equipment swaps. Keeps the flow continuous.
If the seated heels-tucked posture is uncomfortable, sit cross-legged or on a yoga block.
Excellent prep for bent press, windmill, TGU — all the overhead-load KB skills.
